hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 residues, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5, thereby increasing the selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that strong ddG alone is insufficient for success — selectivity (Δmargin > 0) is the primary objective. Future iterations must prioritize disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions with off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore (positions 7-10) or Cys3/Cys14.
